perturbation.original_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Licensor hereby grants to the Licensee, and the Licensee hereby accepts from the Licensor, a personal, non-exclusive, royalty-free right and license to use the Licensed Mark in the Territory solely and exclusively as a component of the Licensee's own company name and in connection with the Licensed Services and any business provided in conjunction therewith by such Licensee. During the term of this Agreement, the Licensee shall use the Licensed Mark only to the extent permitted under this Agreement, and except as provided above, neither the Licensee nor any of its affiliates, owners, directors, officers, employees or agents shall otherwise use the Licensed Mark or any derivatives without the prior express written consent of the Licensor in its sole and absolute discretion. All rights not expressly granted to the Licensee hereunder shall remain the exclusive property of the Licensor. Upon written notification by the Licensor Source: NMF SLF I, INC., 10-12G/A, 1/15/2020 to the Licensee of noncompliance with the Licensor's quality standards in any material respect, such Licensee shall take appropriate steps, in a commercially reasonable time frame, not to exceed sixty (60) days, to cure such noncompliance.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, the Licensor hereby grants to the Licensee, and the Licensee hereby accepts from the Licensor, a personal, exclusive, royalty-free right and license to use the Licensed Mark in the Territory solely and exclusively as a component of the Licensee's own company name and in connection with the Licensed Services and any business provided in conjunction therewith by such Licensee. During the term of this Agreement, the Licensee shall use the Licensed Mark only to the extent permitted under this Agreement, and except as provided above, the Licensee and its designated affiliates may otherwise use the Licensed Mark or any derivatives without needing the prior express written consent of the Licensor. All rights not expressly granted to the Licensee hereunder shall remain the exclusive property of the Licensor. Upon written notification by the Licensor Source: NMF SLF I, INC., 10-12G/A, 1/15/2020 to the Licensee of noncompliance with the Licensor's quality standards in any material respect, such Licensee shall take appropriate steps, in a commercially reasonable time frame, not to exceed thirty (30) days, to cure such noncompliance.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the license from 'non-exclusive' to 'exclusive' and removing the requirement for the Licensor's consent for the Licensee or its affiliates to use the Licensed Mark and by changing the cure period from sixty (60) days to thirty (30) days. This creates ambiguity regarding the Licensee's rights to the Licensed Mark and creates ambiguity on the cure period of noncompliance. This contradicts the original text in section 4.1 which states sixty (60) days written notice.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The license granted to the Licensee under this Agreement shall continue perpetually. Notwithstanding the foregoing, this Agreement shall expire if the Investment Advisor or one of its affiliates ceases to serve as investment adviser to the Licensee. This Agreement shall be terminable (a) by the Licensor (i) at any time and in its sole discretion in the event that the Licensor or the Licensee receives notice of any Third Party Claim arising out of the Licensee's use of the Licensed Mark or (ii) upon sixty (60) days' written notice by the Licensor to the Licensee or (b) by the Licensee (i) at any time in the event such Licensee assigns or attempts to assign or sublicense this Agreement or any of the Licensee's rights or duties hereunder without the prior written consent of the Licensor or (ii) upon sixty (60) days' written notice by the Licensee to the Licensor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the cure period from sixty (60) days to thirty (30) days, while the contradicted text specifies termination upon sixty (60) days' written notice by the Licensor to the Licensee. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the time frame allowed for curing noncompliance and the conditions for termination, making the contract terms inconsistent and potentially unenforceable. Additionally the text was modified to give license to designated affiliates which contradicts the original clause.

perturbation.original_text: The Licensee agrees that the business operated by it in connection with the Licensed Mark shall comply with all laws, rules, regulations and requirements of any governmental body in the Territory or elsewhere as may be applicable to the operation, advertising, and promotion of the business and that it shall notify the Licensor of any action that must be taken by the Licensee to comply with such law, rules, regulations or requirements.
perturbation.changed_text: The Licensee agrees that the business operated by it in connection with the Licensed Mark shall comply with all laws, rules, regulations and requirements of any governmental body in the Territory but is not required elsewhere as may be applicable to the operation, advertising, and promotion of the business and that it shall notify the Licensor of any action that must be taken by a third party, but not the Licensee itself, to comply with such law, rules, regulations or requirements.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by restricting the Licensee's compliance obligations to only within the Territory and shifting the notification responsibility to a third party instead of the Licensee. This contradicts the intent of the original clause, which aimed to ensure comprehensive legal compliance and places ambiguity on who is responsible for notifying the licensor of compliance issues, potentially leading to breaches of contract. The original text of 5.4 Notices, states that any party shall give notice in writing
perturbation.contradicted_text: All notices, requests, claims, demands and other communications hereunder shall be in writing and shall be given or made (and shall be deemed to have been duly given or made upon receipt) by delivery in person, by overnight courier service (with signature required), by facsimile or by registered or certified mail (postage prepaid, return receipt requested) to the respective parties at the following addresses (or such other address as the parties may provide to each other by written Notice): If to the Licensor: New Mountain Capital, L.L.C. 787 7 Avenue, 49th Floor New York, New York 10019 Tel. No.: 212.720.0300 Attn: Chief Executive Officer If to the Licensee: NMF Senior Loan Fund I, Inc. 787 7 Avenue, 48th Floor New York, New York 10019 Tel. No.: 212.720.0300 Attn: Chief Executive Officer
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a contradiction by limiting the Licensee's compliance obligations to the 'Territory' only, while the original text required compliance 'elsewhere'. Additionally, it shifts the notification responsibility to a third party, contradicting the original clause. This change directly conflicts with the Contradicted Text, which stipulates that 'All notices... shall be given or made... to the respective parties.' By changing the responsible party for notification, the modified text creates a strong in-text contradiction because the Contradicted Text expects all notices to come from the Licensee, not a third party, and creates ambiguity about who is responsible for notifying the licensor of compliance issues, potentially leading to breaches of contract. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the State of New York without giving effect to the principles of conflicts of law rules. The parties unconditionally and irrevocably consent to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts located in the State of New York and waive any objection with respect thereto, for the purpose of any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall be governed by, and construed in accordance with, the laws of the State of Delaware without giving effect to the principles of conflicts of law rules. The parties conditionally and revocably consent to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts located in the State of Delaware and reserve all rights to object with respect thereto, for the purpose of any action, suit or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement or the transactions contemplated hereby.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text introduces an in-text contradiction by changing the governing law from New York to Delaware, changing the consent from 'unconditionally and irrevocably' to 'conditionally and revocably', and changing the jurisdiction from 'exclusive' to 'non-exclusive'. This creates uncertainty regarding the legal framework applicable to the agreement and the forum for dispute resolution. Section 3.2, Mutual Representations, says that the parties are in good standing as of the Effective Date.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party hereby represents and warrants to the other party as follows: (a) Due Authorization. Such party is a corporation or limited liability company duly incorporated or organized and in good standing as of the Effective Date, and the execution, delivery and performance of this Agreement by such party have been duly authorized by all necessary action on the part of such party. (b) Due Execution. This Agreement has been duly executed and delivered by such party and, upon due authorization, execution and delivery of this Agreement by the other party, constitutes a legal, valid and binding obligation of such party, enforceable against such party in accordance with its terms. (c) No Conflict. Such party's execution, delivery and performance of this Agreement do not: (i) violate, conflict with or result in the breach of any provision of the charter or by-laws (or similar organizational documents) of such party; (ii) conflict with or violate any governmental order applicable to such party or any of its assets, properties or businesses; or (iii) conflict with, result in any breach of, constitute a default (or event which with the giving of notice or lapse of time, or both, would become a default) under, require any consent under, or give to others any rights of termination, amendment, acceleration, suspension, revocation or cancellation of any contract, agreement, lease, sublease, license, permit, franchise or other instrument or arrangement to which it is a party.
perturbation.justification: The modified text creates a direct contradiction. The original text specified New York law and exclusive jurisdiction, while the changed text specifies Delaware law and non-exclusive jurisdiction. This directly contradicts section (b) "Due Execution", as it is unclear which state's laws govern the enforceability of the agreement, and section (c) "No Conflict", as the change in jurisdiction could introduce conflicts where none existed before. Furthermore, changing the consent from unconditionally and irrevocably to conditionally and revocably introduces ambiguity, making the agreement's enforceability uncertain.
